title: National Strategy for Real-Time PCR Implementation in Foodborne Disease Control
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     diseases     remain     a     significant     public     health     burden  ,   affecting     millions     annually     and     disrupting     food     supply     chains  . Rapid,   accurate     detection     of     pathogens     is     critical     to     minimize     outbreaks     and     protect     public     health  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  ) has   emerged   as   a     powerful     tool  ,   offering     high     sensitivity  ,   specificity  ,   and     rapid     turnaround     compared     to     conventional     culture     methods  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     need     for     a     national     strategy     to     integrate     qPCR     into     routine     food     safety     surveillance  . By   establishing     standardized     protocols  ,   training     laboratory     personnel  ,   and     ensuring     equitable     access     to     qPCR     technology  ,   governments     can     significantly     improve     early     detection  ,   response     speed  ,   and     risk     management  .   A     robust     qPCR     network     will     strengthen     foodborne     disease     control  ,   reduce     economic     losses  ,   and     enhance   global   confidence   in   national     food     safety     systems  .
